nodes.label: P4.3 — width x depth (A4): Scap-budget Pareto, energy-wall baseline, decay-vs-width control
nodes.rationale: A4 cost WIN stands: OURS/OLD backward cost flat in depth while BP grows linearly (52k->169k, up to 6.8x costlier); the fixed-width W64 control proves the droop is depth-decay not the iso-budget width-shrink; contrast composes ~5 layers (energy buys none) then decays.

nodes.label: A4 — the Scap-budget cost WIN: forward-only backward cost flat in depth vs BP's linear growth
nodes.rationale: OURS/OLD backward cost stays flat (~14-40k) as depth grows while tuned-BP's backward cost grows linearly (52k->169k) because BP's credit distance equals full depth; narrow-deep architectures are near-free for forward-only, up to 6.8x costlier for BP at L12.

nodes.label: OLD energy-Sigma-h^2 baseline decisively closed as a depth substrate
nodes.rationale: The energy baseline collapses monotonically with depth (headroom 0.49->0.31) and never composes (probe ~0.37 flat then collapses) — makes the contrast-based depth-wall legible by comparison; energy-Sigma-h^2 sheds whatever isn't loud, so deep layers homogenize (density != class).
